transcript.clean_text: he came he sort the opposition in half and he conquered ramco evan nepal is in charge of this race and by an enormous margin he leads in the overall classification by a mighty two minutes and 41. edward mcmas three minutes and three in ears after a solid time trial by him likewise wrongly as you could say well gc battles put to one side on a flat day today at last one for the sprinters 193 kilometers to cabo couple dagata well wind was going to be a big factor during the day and so it proved for the breakaway brave enough to take it on just three men went up the road no mountains points up for grabs thin pickings you might say boytech raper of kern farmer was up there his team losing three riders before the start john boo of oscar tell us likewise and gets a ball as well other notable riders who didn't take the start simon yates testing positive for covid likewise pavel sivikov and it was thinning down not at this moment with wojtek repper taking a hit on a dusty road blown up by the strong wind that characterized much of the day but certainly we will miss of course julian alaphilippe went down hard damaged his shoulder and now it is another man down for remco ever nepal having already lost peter serry it's been a long road back to recovery for the world champion and he's gone down a side road once again the break started to fracture and the last man standing was yet to both he was to be wound in finally by a pelican determined to have its fun on the day john ball of us kadal asgardi had been brave to get up there in fact all of them had into a headwind that really knocked the pace of the day right back slower than the slowest pace possible forecast it was about to get very quick indeed we came towards the intermediate sprint and the green jersey of matt pederson naturally enough came up to the front and said thank you very much in the absence of sam bennett who went home before the time trial pedersen looks untouchable in the green so it's down to stage win for the fast men and gayden groves team bike exchange were in dominant mood an arrow straight 3.6 kilometer approach meant there were plenty of options out there but also chances to get tangled up many big names found themselves out of position the clay in the bright yellow of archaea samsic looked lively so did john degenkolb who went first but biding their time with those behind uae had gone for milano instead of ackermann but in the end it was kaden groves who paid back his teammates for all their hard work brilliant finish by him danny van popple throwing into second place the man who usually leads out sam bennett just edging dave's favorite tim malia in the tricolor of belgium great finish and a great result for caden groves super work by the sprinters pedersen finishing in fifth place and enhancing his lead once again in that green jersey competition well congratulations to team bike exchange who'd lost simon yates at the top of the day and with gc ambitions put to one side they made the best of a bad situation ever nepal still leads overall by the same margin that he started the day but with a big climb to come on stage 12 up penas blancas could he enhance that many mountains to come we'll have to wait tomorrow before we get to the climb the longest stage of this year's race and a spiky end as well welter never disappoints every day is different and indeed so will be stage 12. we look forward to it
